steps.title: Identify the true subject across the interrupting phrase
steps.content: The subject of the blank is "lost works" (plural). The phrase "no copy of which exists" is a parenthetical relative clause inserted between the subject and the verb; it does not change the subject's number.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement and eliminate
steps.content:
"Lost works" is plural, so the verb must be plural.

A. have become — plural present perfect; agrees with plural "lost works." Correct.
B. becomes — third-person singular present; does not agree with plural subject. Eliminated.
C. has become — third-person singular present perfect; does not agree with plural subject. Eliminated.
D. is becoming — third-person singular progressive; does not agree with plural subject. Eliminated.

A is correct.
